more.title: Exclusive: ZACC investigates Mines Ministry over decision made during Chitando era
more.description: Three months after President Emmerson Mnangagwa removed Winston Chitando as Minister of Mines and Mining Development, the ministry has come under investigation by the Zimbabwe Anti-Corruption Commission (ZACC) over the controversial cancellation of a coal mining concession linked to Sengwa Colliery. more.title: Veteran journalist Jonisayi Maromo dies
more.description: Daily Sun THE African Media and Communicators Forum (AMCF) community is mourning a great loss. Veteran journalist and communicator Jonisayi Maromo passed away early Wednesday morning, 11 March, at Mutare Provincial Hospital in Zimbabwe after a period of illness. According to Elijah Mhlanga, chairman of the AMCF, his passing was confirmed by his wife, Aldrine [...] The post Veteran journalist Jonisayi Maromo dies appeared first on NewZimbabwe.com .

more.title: Dishing out unprotected lula lula on Valentine’s Day leaves extremely sex-starved Murewa woman (24) in jail
more.description: MUREHWA – In a case that has ignited a profound national conversation regarding the protection of minors and the often-overlooked reality of female-on-male sexual abuse, a 24-year-old woman from Murehwa has been sentenced to an effective 26 months in prison. The ruling, handed down by the Murehwa Regional Magistrates’ Court, follows a series of events [...] The post Dishing out unprotected lula lula on Valentine’s Day leaves extremely sex-starved Murewa woman (24) in jail first appeared on My Zimbabwe News .

more.title: Ex-CABS Manager Gets 4 Years In Jail Over US$182,758 Bank Fraud
more.description: Former CABS Manager Jailed For Fraud A former Client Relationship Manager at Central African Building Society (CABS) has been jailed after the Harare Magistrates’ Court sentenced him to an effective four-year prison term for defrauding the bank of US$182,758 (approximately R3,430,000). The court heard that Talent Tarisai Zimbindi abused his position at the financial institution [...] The post Ex-CABS Manager Gets 4 Years In Jail Over US$182,758 Bank Fraud appeared first on iHarare News .
